author: Ameen Hassan
summary: The session involved cleaning up a GitHub repository (data-qualification-agent under ahassan507) that contained 360chi health check report files that had been pushed to GitHub. The user was concerned about the commit history revealing that they had pushed these files. The assistant confirmed the report files (docx, html, json, md formats) were still on disk after a prior git rm --cached operation. The assistant proposed and executed a plan to delete the existing GitHub repo and recreate it with a clean single-commit history to strip all prior git history. The GitHub CLI lacked the delete_repo scope initially, requiring the user to run gh auth refresh. After the user re-authorized, the old repo was deleted and the local git history was stripped via rm -rf .git and git init. The assistant then noticed .claude/settings.local.json was being staged and flagged it as potentially sensitive before completing the commit.
problems: 360chi health check report files had been pushed to a GitHub repo and the user did not want them traceable
Existing repo had commit history referencing ideasorg remote and prior contributors
GitHub CLI lacked delete_repo scope, blocking repo deletion
.claude/settings.local.json and possibly xlsx files were being staged in the fresh commit
approaches: Used git rm --cached to remove files from git tracking without deleting from disk
Proposed replacing repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history
Deleted existing GitHub repo after re-authorizing gh with delete_repo scope
Stripped local git history with rm -rf .git and re-initialized with git init
Flagged .claude/settings.local.json as potentially sensitive before staging
results: Report files confirmed still present on disk after git rm --cached
Local git history stripped and fresh repo initialized
Session ended mid-flow before confirming whether sensitive files were excluded from the fresh commit

decisions.decision: Replace existing repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history
Keep the repo private going forward

open_questions: Were sensitive files like .claude/settings.local.json and any xlsx files properly excluded from the fresh commit?
Was the new GitHub repo created under the same name (data-qualification-agent) or a different one?
Was the fresh commit pushed to GitHub successfully?
